title: Find the diagonal of a rectangle
problem: A rectangle has length $6$ cm and width $8$ cm. Find the length of the diagonal.

steps.instruction: Identify the right-angled triangle. The diagonal of a rectangle is the hypotenuse of a right-angled triangle formed by the length and width.
Take the square root to find the diagonal.

title: Find the distance between two points
problem: Find the distance between the points $(1, 3)$ and $(7, 11)$.

steps.instruction: Find the horizontal distance by subtracting the $x$-coordinates.
Find the vertical distance by subtracting the $y$-coordinates.
These two distances are the legs of a right-angled triangle. Apply Pythagoras' theorem.
Take the square root to find the distance.

title: Real-world problem: ladder against a wall
problem: A ladder $13$ m long leans against a vertical wall. The foot of the ladder is $5$ m from the base of the wall. How high up the wall does the ladder reach?

steps.instruction: Identify the sides of the right-angled triangle. The ladder is the hypotenuse; the wall height and ground distance are the shorter sides.
steps.working: Hypotenuse $= 13$ m, base $= 5$ m, height $= h$.

steps.instruction: Write Pythagoras' theorem with the unknown height.
Take the square root to find the height.
